title: rubus nitidus subsp. affinis
intro: Rubus nitidus subsp. affinis, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus nitidus plant, native to the Himalayan region. It is a shrub with edible fruits that have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus nitidus subsp. affinis include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves, stems, and roots can be used in various herbal preparations to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
